description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a rectangular main body finished in a warm orange-brown color, accented by metallic trim. The front panel features a variety of buttons, switches, and a central analog display, likely for tuning and frequency indication. A classic round microphone on a stand is connected to the main unit, accompanied by a small external speaker box with a visible grille and a control panel. The rear side reveals multiple cable connections, including power and audio cables, as well as warning labels and additional ports. The overall design suggests a robust, utilitarian build, with visible wear and some surface scratches, indicating regular use. The combination of metal, plastic, and electronic components gives it a sturdy, industrial appearance suitable for urban or garage environments.
description: A vintage orange-brown radio receiver with a front control panel, analog display, attached microphone, and external speaker box. The unit features metallic trim, visible cables, and a robust, industrial design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main control panel, analog display, buttons, microphone on a stand, and the external speaker with grille and control knobs.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the main unit and speaker, the microphone's profile, and visible connecting cables.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying multiple cable connections, ports, warning labels, and the back of the speaker and main unit.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the solid side panels, some surface wear, and the arrangement of the microphone and speaker.
